title: Indulgent Egyptian Chestnut Pilaf
description: A salty, pillowy weeknight Egyptian dish featuring chestnuts, tempered spices, and a comforting rice base, kosher-friendly and ready in under an hour.
instructions: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until runoff is clear.
Heat the olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat and add the diced onion, sautéing until translucent.
Add the ground cumin and coriander, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the diced carrot and cook for 2 minutes, then add the pre-soaked chestnuts and stir.
Pour in the chicken broth, add the bay leaf, salt, and pepper, and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice and chestnuts absorb the liquid.
Remove from heat, discard the bay leaf, and let the pilaf rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork, stir in lemon zest, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley or a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
